_meta.description: Mechanical checklist for consolidating 28 CRM tools → 11. One entry per NEW tool.
_meta.scope: ONLY src/lib/runner/tools/crm/. Do NOT touch non-CRM tools — those were aligned in commit 68964bb.
_meta.resume_note: All entries start as TODO. Work through implementation_order. After each tool, run tests, mark DONE.

tools.summary: Move describe_crm_schema from callable tool to system-reminder injection

implementation.what: Inject CRM config (stages, types, roles, industries, custom fields) into the system-reminder block that's built every turn. ~200 bytes of passive context. The agent never has to decide 'should I check the schema?' — it's always there.
notes: The CRM config is already available at runner startup (loaded from DB via CRM_DEFAULTS or client override).
The system-reminder builder is in src/lib/ai/system-prompt.ts — look for where other dynamic context (thread info, timezone, etc.) is injected.
Remove describe_crm_schema from createSchemaTools and from the readTools object in index.ts.

tools.justification: Vercel AGENTS.md study: passive context achieves 100% success vs 53-79% for active retrieval. Costs ~200 bytes vs ~500+ tokens when called as a tool.

tools.summary: Unified search across all CRM entity types (9 old tools → 1)

new_tool.name: search_crm
new_tool.description: Search CRM records. Specify the entity type and optional filters. Returns matching records sorted by relevance. For relationships: use entity 'deal_contacts' with a deal_id or contact_id filter, or filter contacts/deals by company_id. Use this before creating records to check for duplicates.

query.description: Free-text search term. Searches name/address/title/summary fields depending on entity.

filters.description: Key-value filters applied as equality matches. Common filters: { stage: '...', type: '...', status: '...', company_id: '...', contact_id: '...', deal_id: '...' }. For date ranges on interactions: use occurred_after / occurred_before.

new_tool.response_shape: { success: true, records: Record[], count: number } | { success: false, error: string }

implementation.what: Route to the correct table based on entity. Reuse existing query-building logic from each old tool.

deal_contacts.join: contacts(first_name, last_name, email, phone) or deals(deal_id, address, stage, price) depending on which filter is provided
deal_contacts.note: If deal_id filter → join contacts. If contact_id filter → join deals. Reuse logic from get_deal_contacts/get_contact_deals.

reusable_code: buildSearchExpression() from filter-utils.ts — builds OR'd ILIKE expressions
buildIlikePattern() from filter-utils.ts — wraps search term in %...%
normalizeDateString() / normalizeDateUpperBound() from filter-utils.ts — for occurred_after/occurred_before
notes: Always scope by client_id (injected from closure, never in schema).
The filters record maps to .eq() calls. Special-case occurred_after → .gte('occurred_at', ...) and occurred_before → .lte('occurred_at', ...).
query maps to ILIKE search via buildSearchExpression across entity-specific columns.
Return shape is always { records, count } — normalize from entity-specific names (contacts, deals, etc.) to generic 'records'.

tools.summary: Unified record creation with dedup + batch (6 old tools → 1)

new_tool.name: create_record
new_tool.description: Create one or more CRM records. Specify the entity type and field values. Has built-in duplicate detection — if matching records exist, returns possible_duplicates instead of creating. Set force_create: true to override. Supports batch creation (up to 50 records per call). Data Modification Warning: Only create records when the user has explicitly asked.

records.description: Array of records to create. Required fields by entity: contacts: { first_name, last_name }. companies: { name }. deals: { address }. Optional fields vary by entity — use the CRM schema in system context to see available fields, types, industries, stages, and custom fields.

new_tool.response_shape: { success: true, record: Record } (single) | { success: true, records: Record[], count: number } (batch) | { success: false, reason: 'possible_duplicates', possible_duplicates: Record[], message: string } | { success: false, error: string }

implementation.what: Validate records array against entity-specific Zod schema at runtime, run dedup, insert.
notes: For batch (records.length > 1): intra-batch dedup check (same dedup key appears twice) THEN per-entry DB check. Reuse logic from batch_create_contacts.
If records.length === 1: return { record }. If > 1: return { records, count }.
captureServerEvent / captureServerEvents for analytics — reuse same event shape.
Extract findDuplicate* functions into the new file or a shared dedup-utils.ts.

tools.summary: Unified record update with batch support (3 old tools → 1)

new_tool.name: update_record
new_tool.description: Update one or more CRM records by ID. Only provided fields are changed. Pass null to clear a nullable field. Omit fields to leave them unchanged. Custom fields are deep-merged (existing keys not in the patch are preserved). Supports batch updates (up to 50 records per call) — all records must be the same entity type. Data Modification Warning: Only update records when the user has explicitly asked.

updates.type: z.array(z.object({ id: z.string().uuid(), fields: z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()) })).min(1).max(50)
updates.description: Array of { id, fields } patches. All must be the same entity type.

new_tool.response_shape: { success: true, record: Record } (single) | { success: true, records: Record[], count: number } (batch) | { success: false, error: string, results: Array<{ id: string, success: boolean, record?: Record, error?: string }> } (partial failure)

implementation.what: Route to correct table + primary key column based on entity. Validate fields, deep-merge custom fields, execute updates.
notes: Validate each fields object against entity-specific Zod update schema at runtime.
Custom fields deep-merge: reuse mergeCustomFields() from custom-fields.ts.
Always filter by client_id (closure + RLS double-lock).
Batch updates are sequential (not transactional). If one fails mid-batch, return partial results with per-item success/error.

IMPORTANT: deals.ts update_deal has special logic for deal_stage_changed analytics event (lines 193-254). This MUST be preserved in the new tool — when stage changes, capture 'deal_stage_changed' event with from_stage, to_stage, deal_value.

tools.summary: Unified delete with approval gate + audit (5 old tools → 1)

tests_to_rewrite: Tests for individual delete tools in each entity test file

new_tool.name: delete_records
new_tool.description: Permanently delete one or more CRM records by ID. This is irreversible. Supports batch deletion (up to 50 records per call). For deal_contacts links, use link_records with action 'unlink' instead. DESTRUCTIVE: This action requires user approval before execution.

reason.description: Why these records are being deleted. Logged for audit.

new_tool.response_shape: { success: true, deleted_count: number, ids: string[] } | { success: false, error: string, deleted_count: number, failed_ids: string[] }

implementation.what: Delete from correct table based on entity. Always filter by client_id. Log reason for audit.
notes: Set needsApproval: true on the tool definition (same as current per-entity delete tools).
reason field is included in the tool response for audit trail but doesn't affect execution.
Cascade behavior is handled by DB constraints (CASCADE for junction rows, SET NULL for FKs). No application logic needed.
For deal_contacts links: direct users to link_records(action: 'unlink') instead. Don't allow deleting junction rows via this tool.

tools.summary: Unified link/unlink for all CRM relationships (6 old tools → 1)

new_tool.name: link_records
new_tool.description: Create or remove a relationship between two CRM records. Supported relationships: contact↔deal (many-to-many via junction table, with role and primary flag), contact→company (FK on contact), deal→company (FK on deal). Data Modification Warning: Only link/unlink when the user has explicitly asked.

action.description: Whether to create or remove the relationship.

source_id.description: UUID of the source record (contact_id for contact_deal and contact_company, deal_id for deal_company).

target_id.required: Required for 'link'. Omit for 'unlink' on FK relationships.
target_id.description: UUID of the target record (deal_id, company_id).

role.description: Role for contact↔deal links (e.g., buyer, seller, tenant). See CRM schema for valid roles.

is_primary.description: Whether this is the primary contact for display. Only for contact↔deal links.

new_tool.response_shape: { success: true, link: Record } | { success: true, removed: Record } | { success: false, error: string }

implementation.what: Route by relationship type. Junction table ops for contact_deal, FK updates for contact_company and deal_company.

contact_deal.link: INSERT into deal_contacts (client_id, contact_id=source_id, deal_id=target_id, role, is_primary). Validate role against config.deal_contact_roles.
contact_deal.unlink: DELETE from deal_contacts WHERE contact_id=source_id AND deal_id=target_id AND client_id. target_id IS required for unlink on junction table.

contact_company.link: UPDATE contacts SET company_id=target_id WHERE contact_id=source_id AND client_id.
contact_company.unlink: UPDATE contacts SET company_id=NULL WHERE contact_id=source_id AND client_id. target_id not needed.

deal_company.link: UPDATE deals SET company_id=target_id WHERE deal_id=source_id AND client_id.
deal_company.unlink: UPDATE deals SET company_id=NULL WHERE deal_id=source_id AND client_id. target_id not needed.

notes: Role defaults from config: config.deal_contact_roles.includes('buyer') ? 'buyer' : config.deal_contact_roles[0].
For contact_deal unlink: handle PGRST116 (no rows) same as current unlink_contact_from_deal.
Role/is_primary params are only meaningful for contact_deal relationship. Ignore silently for FK relationships.

tools.summary: Read-only SQL escape hatch for complex CRM queries (new)

kills.note: This is a new tool. Does NOT replace run_agent_memory_sql — that stays as the general utility SQL tool.

new_tool.name: crm_sql
new_tool.description: Run a read-only SQL query against CRM tables. Use for complex queries that search_crm cannot express: multi-table JOINs, aggregations (COUNT, SUM, AVG), GROUP BY, HAVING, subqueries, date arithmetic, complex WHERE clauses. Only SELECT and CTE (WITH) queries allowed. Available tables: contacts, companies, deals, interactions, crm_tasks, deal_contacts. All tables have client_id — RLS enforces tenant isolation automatically. Prefer search_crm for simple lookups. Use crm_sql when you need JOINs or aggregations.

query.description: SQL SELECT/CTE query. Single statement only, no semicolons.

purpose.description: Brief description of what this query answers. Logged for audit.

new_tool.response_shape: { success: true, rows: Record[], row_count: number } | { success: false, error: string }

implementation.what: CRM-scoped wrapper around the existing read-only SQL pattern from src/lib/runner/tools/utility/sql.ts.
notes: Reuse the same run_readonly_sql RPC (or equivalent Supabase .rpc() call) used by run_agent_memory_sql.
Validate: must start with SELECT or WITH (case-insensitive), no semicolons, single statement.
purpose field is logged in response but doesn't affect execution.
RLS on all CRM tables enforces client_id scoping — no application-level filter needed for SQL queries.
Add row_count field to response for auto-verification.

tools.justification: Covers complex queries (JOINs, aggregations) that search_crm can't express. Read-only by construction. Separate from run_agent_memory_sql for CRM-specific description and potential future guardrails.

tools.summary: Rewrite index.ts factory to register new consolidated tools

implementation.what: Replace all old tool imports and registrations with the new consolidated tools.
new_imports: createTaskTools from './tasks' (create_task + update_task only — search_tasks removed)
notes: Keep the same CreateCrmToolsOptions interface (mode, config, allowWriteTools, allowDeleteTools).
Keep the setup-mode branch returning createConfigureCrmTool unchanged.
The read/write/delete gating logic stays the same — just fewer tools in each bucket.
interactions.ts and tasks.ts need minor refactors: remove search_* and delete_* exports, keep create/update exports.

tools.summary: Update system prompt and autopilot constants to reference new tool names

files_to_update.change: Line 31: 'search_contacts' → 'search_crm'. Also add system-reminder CRM schema injection (from step 0).

notes: These are string references in prompts/instructions, not code imports.
Grep for any other string references to old tool names across the codebase.

tools.summary: Delete old test files, update integration tests referencing old tool names
notes: Run grep for ALL old tool names across src/ to catch any remaining string references.
Old source files (contacts.ts, companies.ts, deals.ts, deal-contacts.ts, company-links.ts, schema.ts) can be deleted ONLY after the new tools pass all tests and index.ts no longer imports them.
Keep: filter-utils.ts, custom-fields.ts, configure-crm.ts, interactions.ts (modified), tasks.ts (modified).

MODIFY: src/lib/runner/tools/crm/tasks.ts (remove search_tasks + delete_task, keep create_task + update_task)
src/lib/ai/system-prompt.ts (add CRM schema to system-reminder, update tool name references)

safety_checklist: All write tools include 'Data Modification Warning' in description
All tools scope by client_id (closure injection + RLS)
create_record has duplicate detection with force_create override
All mutating tools return full created/updated record for auto-verification
delete_records requires reason field for audit trail
